prerequisites: An SFTP server reachable from Airbyte, with its hostname (host) and port (defaults to 22).
A user account on the SFTP server with a username and password for authentication.
Write permission for that account to the destination_path directory where the JSON Lines files will be written.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the SFTP server using a username and its associated password. Supply the account name in the username field and the secret in the password field; password is the only secret credential.

setupSteps.title: Enter SFTP server connection details
setupSteps.body: Set host to the SFTP server hostname and port to the server port. If your server uses the standard SFTP port, leave port at its default of 22.

setupSteps.title: Provide authentication credentials
setupSteps.body: Set username to the account used to access the SFTP server and password to that account's password. These credentials are used for password-based authentication against the server.

setupSteps.title: Configure the destination path
setupSteps.body: Set destination_path to the directory on the SFTP server where the JSON files will be written. Ensure the configured user has write permission to this directory. Each stream is written as a separate JSON Lines (.jsonl) file under this path.
